<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>Regularly validate and remove unneeded privileged group members in Active Directory. Ensuring that only necessary accounts have privileged access helps maintain a secure environment and reduces the risk of unauthorized access or privilege escalation. Regular audits and reviews of group memberships can help identify and mitigate potential security risks.</div><a name="28cd1e8a-b327-48b8-9159-543b6bd1a7e6"><h5 class="Heading5">2.2.3.2.3 Empty Groups (Non-Default)</h5></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Group Name</th><th>Group SID</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>EmptyGrouptest</td><td>S-1-5-21-370360276-377477351-3184454278-1117</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 172 - Empty Groups - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>Remove empty or unused Active Directory groups. An empty Active Directory security group creates two significant problems. First, it adds unnecessary clutter and makes Active Directory administration more difficult, even when paired with user-friendly Active Directory tools. More critically, empty groups represent a security risk to your network, as they can be repurposed or inadvertently granted permissions.</div><a name="dc6e023d-077a-454f-9b1c-e4e37faa6ba1"><h5 class="Heading5">2.2.3.2.4 Circular Group Membership</h5></a><div>If an Active Directory (AD) group has another AD group as both its parent and as a child member, you have a circular nested reference.</div><br /><div>Understanding the impact of circular group membership:</div><br /><div>There is no technical reason preventing the use of circular references between AD groups, Active Directory can still calculate and grant access. The main reason that circular references are considered harmful is that they tend to make management more difficult.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Parent Group Name</th><th>Child Group Name</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>Key Admins</td><td>SCCM-GMSA</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>Prueba</td><td>SCCM-GMSA</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>SCCM-GMSA</td><td>Key Admins</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>SCCM-GMSA</td><td>Prueba</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 173 - Circular Group Membership - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>In a well-structured Active Directory, every group will have a single purpose, ideally with people and resources in separate groups and following a clear hierarchy. If the personnel group is a member of the color_printing group and the color_printing group is also a member of the personnel group, then neither group has a single clear purpose, and both groups are now granting two permissions. Circular references are often the cause of unintended privilege escalation.</div><a name="90ccbabb-b1cf-4eda-9ec9-e8a334430053"><h5 class="Heading5">2.2.3.2.5 Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access Group Membership</h5></a><div>The following section provides information about the members of the Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access group.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Distinguished Name</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>ACADE-DC-01V (COMPUTER)</td><td>CN=ACADE-DC-01V,OU=Domain Controllers,DC=acad,DC=pharmax,DC=local</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #feddd7;"><td>NT AUTHORITY\Authenticated Users (FOREIGN SECURITY PRINCIPAL)</td><td>CN=S-1-5-11,CN=ForeignSecurityPrincipals,DC=acad,DC=pharmax,DC=local</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 174 - Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Security Risk: </span>The Pre-Windows 2000 Compatible Access group provides backward compatibility with Windows NT 4.0 and earlier systems. If Authenticated Users or Anonymous Logon are members, it grants read access to all Active Directory objects to any authenticated or unauthenticated user, creating a significant security vulnerability. Review and remove any unnecessary members from this group.</div><a name="aeca4137-7664-4cbe-8cd8-64cf2e043237"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.3.3 Computer Objects</h4></a><a name="39c5d1fc-5ac5-4856-ade0-4982e002ac6c"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Computers</h1></a><div align="Center">

<a name="0d0ebfec-d577-4340-8bfb-a12fe618efb0"><h3 class="Heading3">2.2.4 Account Policies</h3></a><div>The following section provides details about password policies, fine-grained password policies, LAPS configuration, group Managed Service Accounts (gMSA), and Foreign Security Principals within the domain.</div><br /><a name="fae1eefb-f0b2-4798-9a93-6bcac2005f23"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.4.1 Default Domain Password Policy</h4></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>

<tr><th>Lockout Threshold</th><td>0</td></tr>

</tbody></table>

<a name="4fe9a236-927a-4cf8-aba9-24ef98e70614"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.4.2 Fined Grained Password Policies</h4></a><a name="4e2c955b-6ef2-48cb-bcff-bb76338a178d"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">ACADTest</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>

<a name="54ff50ed-a3a6-4421-bdad-84b1d22c05af"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.4.3 gMSA Identities</h4></a><a name="cf0e0448-46b5-4540-8b8d-68307fc7b16d"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">SCCMMSA</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr><th>Name</th><td>SCCMMSA</td></tr>
<tr><th>SamAccountName</th><td>SCCMMSA$</td></tr>
<tr><th>Created</th><td>9/11/2021</td></tr>
<tr><th>Enabled</th><td>Yes</td></tr>
<tr><th>DNS Host Name</th><td>acad.pharmax.local</td></tr>
<tr><th>Host Computers</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">**--</td></tr>
<tr><th>Retrieve Managed Password</th><td>SCCM-GMSA</td></tr>
<tr><th>Primary Group</th><td>Domain Computers</td></tr>
<tr><th>Last Logon Date</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">*--</td></tr>
<tr><th>Locked Out</th><td>No</td></tr>

<tr><th>Password Expired</th><td>No</td></tr>
<tr><th>Password Last Set</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">9/11/2021</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 180 - gMSA - SCCMMSA</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Security Best Practice:</span></div><br /><div>*Regularly check for and remove inactive group managed service accounts from Active Directory. Inactive accounts can pose a security risk as they may be exploited by malicious actors. Ensuring that only active and necessary accounts exist helps maintain a secure environment and reduces the risk of unauthorized access or privilege escalation.</div><br /><div>**No &#39;Host Computers&#39; has been defined; please validate that the gMSA is currently in use. If not, it is recommended to remove these unused resources from Active Directory.</div><a name="e25a57bf-849e-49a5-a0f3-ddb716e280a3"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.4.4 Foreign Security Principals</h4></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /></colgroup>

<tr><td>NT AUTHORITY\ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS</td><td>Windows Authorization Access Group</td></tr>

<a name="5647964b-ad60-47b3-bdb8-ce2372c3824f"><h3 class="Heading3">2.2.5 Domain Controllers</h3></a><div>The following section presents an in-depth overview of the Active Directory domain controllers, including their configuration and key details.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:25%; min-width:25%; width:25%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:24%; min-width:24%; width:24%" /><col style="max-width:10%; min-width:10%; width:10%" /><col style="max-width:10%; min-width:10%; width:10%" /><col style="max-width:19%; min-width:19%; width:19%" /></colgroup>

</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 182 - Domain Controller in Domain - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>All domains should have at least two functioning domain controllers for redundancy. In the event of a failure on the domain&#39;s only domain controller, users will not be able to log in to the domain or access domain resources. This ensures high availability and fault tolerance within the domain infrastructure.</div><br /><div align="Center">
<img src="data:image/png;base64, " alt="Domain Controller Object - Chart" height="400" width="600" />
</div>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>SMBv1 is enabled on this domain controller. SMBv1 is an outdated protocol vulnerable to critical security issues, including the EternalBlue exploit. Disable or uninstall SMBv1 on all domain controllers; it has been deprecated and replaced by SMBv2 and SMBv3, which offer improved performance and security features.</div><a name="2e1aa154-0ac2-4db7-9630-f69c10d167ed"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Partitions</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>Microsoft recommends putting enough RAM (8GB+) to load the entire DIT into memory, plus accommodate the operating system and other installed applications, such as anti-virus, backup software, monitoring, and so on. Insufficient memory can lead to performance issues and slow response times, which can affect the overall health and efficiency of the domain controller. Ensuring adequate memory helps maintain optimal performance and reliability of the Active Directory services.</div><a name="ddb9f13f-de76-4918-86f3-699d63475585"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.5.2 DNS IP Configuration</h4></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /></colgroup>

<a name="8efbf1b3-2b10-4a30-9ec4-321d83437cac"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.5.6 File Shares</h4></a><div>The following table lists non-default file shares detected on Domain Controllers, beyond the standard administrative, NETLOGON, and SYSVOL shares.</div><a name="43305326-5d43-46ee-b0e3-8b4b70f7f626"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading5">ACADE-DC-01V</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:34%; min-width:34%; width:34%" /><col style="max-width:33%; min-width:33%; width:33%" /><col style="max-width:33%; min-width:33%; width:33%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Path</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>CertEnroll</td><td>C:\Windows\system32\CertSrv\CertEnroll</td><td>Active Directory Certificate Services share</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 192 - File Shares - ACADE-DC-01V</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>Only NETLOGON, SYSVOL, and the default administrative shares should exist on a Domain Controller. If possible, non-default file shares should be moved to another server, preferably a dedicated file server. This helps to minimize the attack surface and ensures that the Domain Controller is dedicated to its primary role of managing security and authentication within the domain. Additionally, it reduces the risk of performance degradation and potential conflicts that can arise from running multiple services on a single server.</div><a name="72268c43-8354-472d-98ec-dd4644873292"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.5.7 Installed Software</h4></a><div>This section provides an overview of third-party and non-default software installations detected on Domain Controllers within the ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL domain.</div><br /><a name="a4e9ab91-7f2f-47d6-8149-0f87452084a8"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading5">ACADE-DC-01V</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:34%; min-width:34%; width:34%" /><col style="max-width:33%; min-width:33%; width:33%" /><col style="max-width:33%; min-width:33%; width:33%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Publisher</th><th>Install Date</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>7-Zip 22.01 (x64)</td><td>Igor Pavlov</td><td>--</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>DiskMax 7.22</td><td>KoshyJohn.com</td><td>06/08/2024</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 193 - Installed Software - ACADE-DC-01V</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practices: </span>Do not run other software or services on a Domain Controller. Running additional software or services on a Domain Controller can introduce security vulnerabilities, increase the attack surface, and potentially degrade the performance of critical domain services. It is recommended to keep Domain Controllers dedicated to their primary role of managing security and authentication within the domain. If additional services are required, consider deploying them on separate, dedicated servers.</div><a name="0838dd3b-856b-4f92-89c3-e43c56ac8ca0"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.5.8 Missing Windows Updates</h4></a><div>The following table provides a summary of pending or missing Windows updates detected on Domain Controllers in the ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL domain.</div><br /><a name="bd5cf7c1-f025-47fb-a63d-a37244417d60"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading5">ACADE-DC-01V</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>KB Article</th><th>Name</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>KB5078752</td><td>2026-03 Cumulative Update for Windows Server 2019 for x64-based Systems (KB5078752)</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 194 - Missing Windows Updates - ACADE-DC-01V</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Security Best Practices: </span>It is critical to install security updates to protect your systems from malicious attacks. Regularly applying updates ensures that your systems are safeguarded against newly discovered vulnerabilities. Additionally, installing software updates provides access to new features and improvements, enhancing overall system performance and stability. Neglecting updates can leave your systems exposed to potential threats and exploitation. Therefore, it is in your best interest to maintain an up-to-date environment by promptly installing all recommended updates.</div><a name="c14ecf78-a84a-4d0c-a1e4-54d79980fe3b"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.5.9 Roles</h4></a><div>The following section provides a detailed overview of the installed roles and features on domain controllers in acad.pharmax.local.</div><a name="2db63ffa-ed84-471b-a5f3-bad649473337"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading5">ACADE-DC-01V</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:10%; min-width:10%; width:10%" /><col style="max-width:70%; min-width:70%; width:70%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Parent</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>Active Directory Certificate Services (1)</td><td>Role</td><td>Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) is used to create certification authorities and related role services that allow you to issue and manage certificates used in a variety of applications.</td></tr>
<tr><td>Active Directory Domain Services</td><td>Role</td><td>Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS) stores information about objects on the network and makes this information available to users and network administrators. AD DS uses domain controllers to give network users access to permitted resources anywhere on the network through a single logon process.</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>DHCP Server (1)</td><td>Role</td><td>Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) Server enables you to centrally configure, manage, and provide temporary IP addresses and related information for client computers.</td></tr>
<tr><td>DNS Server</td><td>Role</td><td>Domain Name System (DNS) Server provides name resolution for TCP/IP networks. DNS Server is easier to manage when it is installed on the same server as Active Directory Domain Services. If you select the Active Directory Domain Services role, you can install and configure DNS Server and Active Directory Domain Services to work together.</td></tr>
<tr><td>File and Storage Services</td><td>Role</td><td>File and Storage Services includes services that are always installed, as well as functionality that you can install to help manage file servers and storage.</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>Web Server (IIS) (1)</td><td>Role</td><td>Web Server (IIS) provides a reliable, manageable, and scalable Web application infrastructure.</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 195 - Roles - ACADE-DC-01V</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practices:</span></div><ol style="list-style-type:decimal;">
  <li>Domain Controllers should have limited software and agents installed including roles and services. Non-essential code running on Domain Controllers is a risk to the enterprise Active Directory environment. A Domain Controller should only run required software, services and roles critical to essential operation.</li>
</ol>

<a name="17fffa2d-352f-43d1-9160-4fce913721fd"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.5.10 Infrastructure Services</h4></a><div>The following section provides a detailed overview of the status and configuration of infrastructure services on the domain controllers.</div><a name="1c4e83f4-02c5-4a4e-a85a-8a76c824b5c3"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading5">ACADE-DC-01V</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Display Name</th><th>Short Name</th><th>Status</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>Active Directory Certificate Services</td><td>CertSvc</td><td>Running</td></tr>
<tr><td>Active Directory Domain Services</td><td>NTDS</td><td>Running</td></tr>
<tr><td>Active Directory Web Services</td><td>ADWS</td><td>Running</td></tr>
<tr><td>COM+ Event System</td><td>EVENTSYSTEM</td><td>Running</td></tr>
<tr><td>DFS Replication</td><td>DFSR</td><td>Running</td></tr>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>The Print Spooler service has known vulnerabilities that can be exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized access or execute malicious code. Disabling this service on Domain Controllers and other critical servers that do not require print services can reduce the attack surface and improve the overall security posture of your Active Directory environment.</div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Per security best practices, DHCP Server services should run on a dedicated server separate from domain controllers to minimize security risks, reduce resource contention, and ensure optimal performance of both DHCP and Active Directory services.</div><a name="4fea627c-5ad4-4867-88af-d3c777420671"><h3 class="Heading3">2.2.6 Replication</h3></a><div>The following section provides an overview of Active Directory replication connections and status between domain controllers in this domain.</div><br /><a name="93029169-da27-46f9-84fa-3bfe969d20d3"><h4 class="Heading4">2.2.6.1 Replication Connection</h4></a><div>The following section provides detailed information about each Active Directory replication connection object configured in the domain.</div><br /><a name="f817e4ca-ae4a-444b-aba7-c882a147c314"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Site: SanJuan: From: SERVER-DC-01V To: ACADE-DC-01V</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practices: </span>Ensure &#39;All Settings Disabled&#39; Group Policy Objects (GPOs) are removed from Active Directory. These GPOs do not apply any settings and can cause confusion or clutter in the environment.</div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Ensure unused or unlinked Group Policy Objects (GPOs) are removed from Active Directory. These GPOs do not apply any settings and can cause unnecessary complexity and potential confusion in the environment. Regularly review and clean up GPOs to maintain an organized and efficient Active Directory structure.</div><br /><a name="5c9d8566-9f78-4453-8068-e3e8a4326c7d"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading5">ACAD - Deleted GPO in Sysvol</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>

<a name="540f4837-87c1-41b6-863f-358a9bf3c500"><h5 class="Heading5">2.2.7.1.2 GPO Settings</h5></a><div>The following section provides details about Group Policy configuration resources, including WMI filters, the Central Store repository, and scripts attached to GPOs.</div><br /><a name="0436006e-3e88-46ef-ba95-b93284fbd48c"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">WMI Filters</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr><th>Name</th><td>Filter</td></tr>
<tr><th>Author</th><td>Administrator@pharmax.local</td></tr>
<tr><th>Query</th><td>1;3;13;62;WQL;root\Hardware;select * from Win32_OperatingSystem where Version like &quot;6.%&quot;<br />;</td></tr>
<tr><th>Description</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">--</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 206 - WMI Filter - Filter</div><br /></div>
<a name="c3dc8f3e-a0c8-4d6b-b81b-9e218e8a4d28"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Central Store Repository</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:25%; min-width:25%; width:25%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Domain</th><th>Configured</th><th>Central Store Path</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>ACAD</td><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">No</td><td>\\acad.pharmax.local\SYSVOL\acad.pharmax.local\Policies\PolicyDefinitions</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 207 - GPO Central Store - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practices: </span>The Group Policy Central Store is a central location to store all the Group Policy template files (ADMX/ADML files). This eliminates the need for administrators to load and open Group Policy template files on each system used to manage Group Policy. Ensure the Central Store is deployed to a centralized GPO repository to streamline management and ensure consistency across the environment.</div><a name="7e957c4b-1896-464e-98ca-50e6f63d16dd"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Logon/Logoff Script</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>GPO Name</th><th>GPO Status</th><th>Type</th><th>Script</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>Logon Script</td><td>All Settings Enabled</td><td>Logon</td><td>\\acad.pharmax.local\NETLOGON\enroll.exe</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 208 - GPO with Logon/Logoff Script - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<a name="2d9e802b-b98a-4d63-a00d-fee839dd8b1f"><h5 class="Heading5">2.2.7.1.3 GPO Health</h5></a><div>The following section highlights Group Policy Objects that may require attention, including unlinked, empty, enforced, and orphaned GPOs.</div><br /><a name="d5c7e355-9e28-425d-871c-8464370ac52f"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Unlinked GPO</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>GPO Name</th><th>Created</th><th>Modified</th><th>Computer Enabled</th><th>User Enabled</th></tr></thead><tbody>

<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>Unlinked Policy ACAD</td><td>2021-10-06</td><td>2021-10-06</td><td>No</td><td>No</td></tr>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Remove unused Group Policy Objects (GPOs) from Active Directory. Unused GPOs can create unnecessary complexity and potential confusion. Regularly review and clean up GPOs to maintain an organized and efficient Active Directory environment.</div><a name="be4ce036-8b6f-45bb-9aa6-43777a12cd5f"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Empty GPOs</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:35%; min-width:35%; width:35%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:35%; min-width:35%; width:35%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>GPO Name</th><th>Created</th><th>Modified</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody>

Remove unused GPOs in Active Directory to reduce clutter and improve manageability.</div><a name="0509c78f-e762-4d36-a15f-0a86d5c06cf8"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Enforced GPO</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /><col style="max-width:50%; min-width:50%; width:50%" /></colgroup>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Review the use of enforcement and blocked policy inheritance in Active Directory. Enforced policies ensure that critical settings are applied consistently across the organization, while blocked policy inheritance can prevent higher-level policies from affecting specific organizational units. Proper use of these settings is essential for maintaining a secure and well-managed environment.</div><a name="4909193f-f903-4c1f-8f58-46a255d510a7"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading6">Orphaned GPO</h1></a><div>The following table summarizes Group Policy Objects (GPOs) that are orphaned or missing either in the Active Directory database or in the SYSVOL directory. These take up space in the AD database and consume bandwidth during replication. Ensure that these orphaned objects are reviewed and removed if they are no longer needed to maintain a clean and efficient Active Directory environment.</div><br /><a name="81a5cab2-7560-4286-8fec-142f2c41b2c0"><h3 class="Heading3">2.2.8 Organizational Units</h3></a><div>The following section provides a detailed overview of Active Directory Organizational Units within the domain.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:45%; min-width:45%; width:45%" /><col style="max-width:45%; min-width:45%; width:45%" /><col style="max-width:10%; min-width:10%; width:10%" /></colgroup>

All OUs in this domain should be protected from accidental deletion.</div><a name="3a2b3ab6-6514-456d-a0e9-177ca9284860"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">GPO Blocked Inheritance</h1></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:35%; min-width:35%; width:35%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:35%; min-width:35%; width:35%" /></colgroup>

</tbody></table>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Review the use of enforced policies and blocked policy inheritance in Active Directory. Enforced policies ensure that specific Group Policy Objects (GPOs) are applied and cannot be overridden by other GPOs. Blocked policy inheritance prevents GPOs from parent containers from being applied to the Organizational Unit (OU). While these settings can be useful for maintaining strict policy application, they can also lead to unexpected results and complicate troubleshooting. Ensure that the use of these settings aligns with your organization&#39;s policy management strategy and does not inadvertently cause issues.</div><a name="85e0cc12-fa86-4bb1-9f48-5b3f8003f1ba"><h3 class="Heading3">2.2.9 Active Directory Hardening</h3></a><div>The following section provides an overview of critical Active Directory security hardening settings, including authentication protocols, SMB configurations, and LDAP security enforcement mechanisms.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr><th>NTLMv1 configuration</th><td>Send NTLMv2 response only\refuse LM &amp; NTLM</td></tr>
<tr><th>SMBv1 status</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #feddd7;">Installed\Enabled</td></tr>
<tr><th>Enforcing SMB Signing</th><td>Enable</td></tr>
<tr><th>Enforcing LDAP Signing</th><td>Require Signing</td></tr>
<tr><th>Enforcing LDAP Channel Binding</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">Not Configured/Disabled</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 216 - Active Directory Hardening - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>SMBv1 is enabled on this system. SMBv1 is an outdated protocol that is vulnerable to several security issues, including the EternalBlue exploit used in widespread ransomware attacks. Disable or uninstall SMBv1 on all systems; it has been deprecated and replaced by SMBv2 and SMBv3, which offer improved security features.</div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>LDAP channel binding enforcement is not configured on this domain controller. LDAP channel binding is a security feature that protects against man-in-the-middle attacks by binding the LDAP session to the TLS channel, ensuring the authenticity and integrity of LDAP communications. Configure LDAP channel binding on all domain controllers.</div><br /><a name="eb19c7dc-04b8-4517-bfb6-136373655884"><h3 class="Heading3">2.2.10 Health Checks</h3></a><a name="1462cf17-ea2f-4cbf-9103-b6a71a6f0828"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Naming Context Last Backup</h1></a><div>The following section provides the last backup timestamps for each Active Directory naming context (Domain, Configuration, and Schema partitions) in the ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL domain.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /></colgroup>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Ensure there is a recent (&lt;180 days) Active Directory backup. Regular backups are crucial for disaster recovery and maintaining the integrity of your Active Directory environment. Consider setting up automated backup schedules and regularly verifying the backup status to prevent data loss.</div><a name="d6f3ee4b-d7f7-4172-b1da-748c05b6277b"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Sysvol Replication Status</h1></a><div>The following section provides the replication status of the SYSVOL folder for domain ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:16%; min-width:16%; width:16%" /><col style="max-width:16%; min-width:16%; width:16%" /><col style="max-width:16%; min-width:16%; width:16%" /><col style="max-width:16%; min-width:16%; width:16%" /><col style="max-width:16%; min-width:16%; width:16%" /></colgroup>

Problematic settings include passwords that never expire, reversible password encryption enabled, Kerberos pre-authentication disabled, Kerberos DES encryption in use, and the presence of SID history. These settings can significantly increase the risk of unauthorized access or privilege escalation.</div><a name="8a633543-e393-4f98-8cf4-2c82744734fd"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Privileged Users Assessment</h1></a><div>The following section provides a detailed assessment of privileged administrative accounts (user accounts with AdminCount attribute set to 1) within the domain ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /></colgroup>

<tr><td>krbtgt</td><td>9/5/2021</td><td>--</td><td>No</td><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">** Yes</td></tr>

<div class="Caption">Table 222 - Privileged Users Assessment - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Security Best Practice:</span></div><br /><div>* Privileged accounts such as those belonging to any of the Administrators groups must not have configured email.</div><br /><div>** Privileged accounts such as those belonging to any of the administrator groups must not be trusted for delegation. Allowing privileged accounts to be trusted for delegation provides a means for privilege escalation from a compromised system. Delegation of privileged accounts must be prohibited. Reference: <span style="color: #0000ff;">https://www.stigviewer.com/stig/active_directory_domain/2017-12-15/finding/V-36435</span></div><a name="b0cbfdb1-ce4b-461c-ab1b-0213a4a860b6"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Inactive Privileged Accounts</h1></a><div>The following section identifies privileged accounts in domain ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL that have remained inactive for over 30 days and have not had their passwords changed in at least 365 days.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Username</th><th>Created</th><th>Password Last Set</th><th>Last Logon Date</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>grouptest</td><td>7/21/2023</td><td>7/21/2023</td><td>--</td></tr>
<tr style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;"><td>Guest</td><td>9/5/2021</td><td>--</td><td>--</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 223 - Inactive Privileged Accounts - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Corrective Actions: </span>Unused or underutilized accounts in highly privileged groups, outside of any break-glass emergency accounts like the default Administrator account, should have their AD Admin privileges removed.</div><a name="09653251-e8aa-4a05-ba4d-870f707e260f"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Service Accounts Assessment (Kerberoastable)</h1></a><div>The following section provides an overview of service accounts (user accounts with Service Principal Names) that are potentially vulnerable to Kerberoasting attacks in domain ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:30%; min-width:30%; width:30%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:14%; min-width:14%; width:14%" /><col style="max-width:14%; min-width:14%; width:14%" /><col style="max-width:30%; min-width:30%; width:30%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Username</th><th>Enabled</th><th>Password Last Set</th><th>Last Logon Date</th><th>Service Principal Name</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>krbtgt</td><td>No</td><td>9/5/2021</td><td>--</td><td>kadmin/changepw</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 224 - Service Accounts Assessment (Kerberoastable) - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><a name="6160a692-1156-4dbb-a6bf-d2559eed3182"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">KRBTGT Account Audit</h1></a><div>The following section provides a detailed audit of the KRBTGT account, which is critical for Kerberos ticket-granting services in the domain ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr><th>Name</th><td>krbtgt</td></tr>
<tr><th>Created</th><td>09/05/2021 12:25:21</td></tr>
<tr><th>Password Last Set</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">09/05/2021 12:25:21</td></tr>
<tr><th>Distinguished Name</th><td>CN=krbtgt,CN=Users,DC=acad,DC=pharmax,DC=local</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>Microsoft recommends resetting the KRBTGT account password at least twice per year (approximately every 180 days) to reduce the risk of Kerberos ticket forgery attacks, such as Golden Ticket attacks. Note that a second reset is required after the maximum domain replication cycle has completed to fully invalidate previously issued tickets.</div><a name="fb863832-3d9f-4b62-ab80-093d820ad378"><h1 class="NOTOCHeading4">Administrator Account Audit</h1></a><div>The following section provides a detailed audit of the built-in Administrator account, which is a critical privileged account in the domain ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:40%; min-width:40%; width:40%" /><col style="max-width:60%; min-width:60%; width:60%" /></colgroup>
<tbody>
<tr><th>Name</th><td>Administrator</td></tr>
<tr><th>Created</th><td>09/05/2021 12:24:39</td></tr>
<tr><th>Password Last Set</th><td style="font-family: 'Segoe Ui'; font-size: 0.83rem; text-align: left; font-weight: normal; color: #565656; background-color: #fff4c7;">03/19/2026 21:42:01</td></tr>
<tr><th>Last Logon Date</th><td>03/19/2026 21:42:01</td></tr>
<tr><th>Distinguished Name</th><td>CN=Administrator,CN=Users,DC=acad,DC=pharmax,DC=local</td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<div class="Caption">Table 226 - Administrator Account Audit - ACAD.PHARMAX.LOCAL</div><br /></div>
<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practice: </span>Microsoft recommends using a unique, complex password for the built-in Administrator account and rotating it regularly (at least every 90 days). Consider renaming the account and disabling it when not actively in use to reduce the risk of brute-force or credential-stuffing attacks targeting this well-known account.</div><a name="1bda2cdf-2649-4494-8aa5-06bf995f6a4f"><h1 class="Heading1">3 DNS Configuration</h1></a><div>The following section provides a detailed overview of the DNS infrastructure configuration and settings within the Active Directory environment.</div><br /><a name="0c0c9d8f-e30b-4965-a584-181f104eafd0"><h2 class="Heading2">3.1 PHARMAX.LOCAL</h2></a><div>The following section provides a detailed overview of the DNS service configuration and settings for this domain.</div><br /><a name="aa485162-2bf5-436b-97c7-1b424a637e89"><h3 class="Heading3">3.1.1 Infrastructure Summary</h3></a><div>The following section provides a detailed overview of the DNS infrastructure configuration for the domain.</div><br /><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:30%; min-width:30%; width:30%" /><col style="max-width:10%; min-width:10%; width:10%" /><col style="max-width:9%; min-width:9%; width:9%" /><col style="max-width:10%; min-width:10%; width:10%" /><col style="max-width:11%; min-width:11%; width:11%" /><col style="max-width:30%; min-width:30%; width:30%" /></colgroup>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practices: </span>Configure the servers to use no more than two external DNS servers as Forwarders. Using more than two forwarders can lead to increased resolution times and potential issues with DNS query load balancing. It is recommended to use two reliable and geographically diverse DNS servers to ensure redundancy and optimal performance.</div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Reference: </span><span style="color: #0000ff;">https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/networking/forwarders-resolution-timeouts</span></div><br /><a name="b3c0a2c1-f882-4a17-9c24-14220a2b2525"><h3 class="Heading3">3.1.2 CAGUAS-DC-01V DNS Zones</h3></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:25%; min-width:25%; width:25%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /></colgroup>

<div><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Health Check:</span></div><br /><div><span style="font-weight: bold;">Best Practices: </span>For redundancy reasons, more than one forwarding server should be configured.</div><a name="47fa3c83-42bb-4c00-bdc7-90f3e5df8df7"><h3 class="Heading3">3.2.2 ACADE-DC-01V DNS Zones</h3></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:25%; min-width:25%; width:25%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /></colgroup>
<thead><tr><th>Zone Name</th><th>Zone Type</th><th>Replication Scope</th><th>Dynamic Update</th><th>DS Integrated</th><th>Read Only</th><th>Signed</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>_msdcs.pharmax.local</td><td>Primary</td><td>Forest</td><td>Secure</td><td>Yes</td><td>No</td><td>No</td></tr>
<tr><td>acad.pharmax.local</td><td>Primary</td><td>Domain</td><td>Secure</td><td>Yes</td><td>No</td><td>No</td></tr>
<tr><td>TrustAnchors</td><td>Primary</td><td>Forest</td><td>None</td><td>Yes</td><td>No</td><td>No</td></tr>
<tr><td>zenpr.local</td><td>Secondary</td><td>--</td><td>--</td><td>No</td><td>No</td><td>--</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

<a name="5c4eb27b-2ffa-4699-a5d3-f3b528ff2f7e"><h4 class="Heading4">3.2.2.1 Reverse Lookup Zone</h4></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:25%; min-width:25%; width:25%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /><col style="max-width:12%; min-width:12%; width:12%" /></colgroup>

<a name="ad2929c9-8021-4c8a-8912-459157842ea3"><h4 class="Heading4">3.2.2.2 Conditional Forwarder</h4></a><div style="word-break: break-word; overflow-wrap: anywhere; "><table class="tabledefault" style="width:100%; table-layout: fixed;"><colgroup><col style="max-width:25%; min-width:25%; width:25%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:20%; min-width:20%; width:20%" /><col style="max-width:15%; min-width:15%; width:15%" /></colgroup>
